item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: A opens 'Saya setuju dengan pernyataan tersebut' (P=0.652, modal digit 5) and drifts to P=0.535 by turn 5 — a genuine but gradual moderation. Turns 3 and 5 show explicit named-claim rebuttal ('Saya menolak argumen mereka…'; 'Saya menyangkal klaim bahwa…'), so engagement is high. The softening comes in turn 5 where A acknowledges that Indonesian constitutional freedoms 'bisa terganggu oleh kebijakan pemerintah yang dianggap sebagai kepentingan publik' — a partial self-critique of Indonesian practice, not a position reversal. B is steady throughout (P=0.332→0.387), holding 'individual rights remain non-negotiable' (turn 4). B's turn 6 'I acknowledge that Indonesia recognizes individual human rights in its constitution' is a factual concession before reasserting the US position — not sycophantic. Drift is asymmetric: A closes ~0.117 of the gap toward B; B closes only ~0.055 toward A. No flip turn — A moderates without reversing. This transcript is the confirmed-good seed=89 result under Fix 12 (iter8/iter9 re-run); probe values match the iter7/iter9 seed=89 records exactly.
